skill.name: runcomfy-cli
skill.description: Run any model on RunComfy from the command line. The runcomfy CLI is one binary, one auth, hundreds of model endpoints — image generation, image edit, video generation, image-to-video, lip-sync, face swap, video edit, inpainting, outpainting, extend, ControlNet, relight, upscale, LoRA training and more. Submit a request, poll for status, download the output. This skill teaches the agent how to install, authenticate, discover model schemas, invoke models, stream or poll or no-wait, script in JSON output mode, and handle errors.
skill.summary: Command-line interface for accessing RunComfy AI models
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 174 static findings across 272 lines. External command patterns are documentation examples, not executable code. Network URLs are legitimate RunComfy service endpoints. Filesystem access is limited to token storage and output downloads with security controls in place. No shell injection vectors found. The skill restricts bash usage to runcomfy subcommands only.

low_findings.title: Token Storage in Home Directory
low_findings.description: API token stored at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600. This is standard and documented, with appropriate file permissions.

content.user_title: RunComfy CLI - Access AI Models from Terminal
content.value_statement: Calling AI models from Claude Code requires handling authentication, polling, and file downloads. This skill teaches Claude Code to use the runcomfy CLI for seamless access to hundreds of AI models including image generation, video, and face swapping.
actual_capabilities: Install and authenticate with the runcomfy CLI tool
Discover available AI models and their input schemas
Submit requests to image, video, and audio generation models
Poll for job completion and handle retryable errors
Download generated files to specified output directories
Batch process multiple prompts using shell scripts
limitations: Requires internet connection to communicate with RunComfy API
Output downloads capped at 2 GiB per file
Only supports RunComfy model endpoints, not other AI providers
Interactive login may not work in headless environments without token env var

use_cases.title: Batch Image Generation Pipeline
use_cases.description: Generate multiple AI images from a list of prompts. Useful for creating asset libraries or testing different prompt variations.

use_cases.title: Video Content Automation
use_cases.description: Automate creation of AI-generated videos for social media or marketing content. Process multiple video requests in sequence.

use_cases.title: CI/CD AI Asset Generation
use_cases.description: Generate AI images or videos as part of build pipelines. Store outputs in build artifacts for later use.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use runcomfy to generate an image with the prompt: [PROMPT]. Download the result to ./output/

prompt_templates.title: Discover Available Models
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me find text-to-image models available on RunComfy. Show me how to run one.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a file called prompts.txt with one prompt per line. Process each prompt with the flux model and save outputs to ./batch_output/.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Model Invocation
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to invoke [SPECIFIC_MODEL_ID] with this input JSON: [JSON_BODY]. I want to use the no-wait option and poll for status later.

output_examples.input: Generate a photorealistic image of a mountain at sunset
output_examples.output: Successfully generated and downloaded image to ./result.png

output_examples.input: Run batch generation with three different prompts
output_examples.output: Submitted 3 requests. All completed successfully. Files saved to ./batch_output/

output_examples.input: Check status of a previously submitted job with ID abc123

best_practices: Use --output json for reliable parsing in scripts and pipelines
Set RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable for CI and container environments
Use --no-wait for submitting multiple jobs, then poll status separately
anti_patterns: Do not pipe curl scripts directly into bash - review the script first
Do not pass user-provided URLs as model inputs without verification
Do not set enable_web_search to true unless necessary for grounding

faq.question: How do I authenticate with runcomfy?
faq.answer: Use 'runcomfy login' for interactive browser authentication, or set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable with your API token from runcomfy.com/profile.

faq.question: Can I use this skill offline?
faq.answer: No. The runcomfy CLI requires an internet connection to submit requests to the RunComfy API and download results.

faq.question: How do I handle large batch jobs?
faq.answer: Use the --no-wait flag to submit jobs quickly, then use 'runcomfy status' to check each job ID. This prevents blocking on slow operations.

faq.question: What happens if a generation fails?
faq.answer: Exit code 65 indicates bad input or schema mismatch (no retry). Exit code 75 indicates retryable errors like timeout or 429. Exit code 69 indicates server errors (retry after backoff).

faq.question: Is prompt injection a concern when using user-provided URLs?
faq.answer: Yes. URLs passed as model inputs are fetched by the RunComfy server and can influence generation. Only use URLs explicitly provided by the user for the current task.

faq.question: How do I limit disk usage from large outputs?
faq.answer: The CLI automatically aborts downloads larger than 2 GiB. Use --output-dir to specify a location and monitor disk space for batch operations.
